articles.title: Everett woman arrested after dog attack injures pet and owner
articles.description:
The 49-year-old woman was arrested on several outstanding warrants, while her dog will be placed in quarantine following the alleged attack, police say.

The post Everett woman arrested after dog attack injures pet and owner appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Everett woman, 49, arrested after her dog attacked a pet and owner.
The woman had several outstanding warrants at the time of her arrest.
The dog will be quarantined, affecting local pet safety measures.

articles.title: Wife of Maine father who died saving his children opens up about their ‘love story’
articles.description:
Ryan Jennings, 46, drowned on April 1, while rescuing two of his children from a Florida riptide.

The post Wife of Maine father who died saving his children opens up about their ‘love story’ appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Ryan Jennings, 46, drowned on April 1 while rescuing his children in Florida.
His wife shared their love story, highlighting their family's bond and tragedy.
Boston residents reflect on the risks of water safety during family vacations.

articles.title: A Vermont ski lift is being retired after six decades
articles.description:
"We know this one will hit home for a lot of people."

The post A Vermont ski lift is being retired after six decades appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Vermont's six-decade-old ski lift will be retired, affecting local skiers' experiences.
The lift has served generations of skiers since its installation in the 1960s.
Boston residents may seek alternative ski destinations as local favorites change.

articles.title: Ask the gardener: Will your new yard be an immediate oasis or a long-term headache?
articles.description:
Just as a home can be turnkey, “cues for care” in the garden can indicate whether the yard is good to go.

The post Ask the gardener: Will your new yard be an immediate oasis or a long-term headache? appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Evaluate garden cues to determine immediate usability for new homeowners.
Consider long-term maintenance needs for shrubs and trees in Boston yards.
Proper assessment can prevent future gardening headaches for residents.
